$description: BIM WATER MANAGEMENT token category — site water management, stormwater detention, and sustainable landscaping metrics for the Professional Centre and Suburban Office site type. These tokens represent a sample site analysis illustrating the site detention and infiltration system design for a building with a 61,200 SF roof curtain area. Source: AEC_Sample Water Management System.pdf (Collaborators #11, 2025-04-01); AEC_Sample Architects_Landscaping and Parking_V3.pdf (Collaborators #27, 2025-05-06). Volume values are in US gallons (authoritative for this dataset); area values in SF. Intended as reference parameters for site design tokens in IFC IfcSite context.

site-water.$description: Site-level water flow calculations for a sample Professional Centre site. All values are annual (GAL/YR).

total-treated-runoff.$description: 2,065,937 GAL/YR — total water volume from 100% treated runoff on sample site. Includes all impervious and permeable surface contributions.

buffalo-grass-runoff.$description: 1,858,974 GAL/YR — annual runoff from Buffalo Grass landscaped areas. Buffalo Grass (Bouteloua dactyloides) is a drought-tolerant native prairie grass used as permaculture landscaping in Professional Centre sites.

tidal-water-storage.$description: 409,063 GAL/YR — tidal water storage volume. Represents captured stormwater retained in site detention and infiltration systems before release or reuse.

site-area.$description: Site surface areas driving stormwater calculations.

building-roof-curtain.$description: 61,200 SF (5,685.12 m²) — building roof curtain area contributing to stormwater runoff. Primary impervious surface on site.

permeable-pavers.$description: 31,407 SF (2,917.05 m²) — permeable paver area. Reduces effective impervious surface coverage; allows stormwater infiltration to sub-base and underground gallery.

infiltration-gallery.$description: Underground stormwater infiltration gallery — captures, stores, and infiltrates site runoff to reduce peak discharge and support landscaping irrigation.

capacity.$description: 258,800 gallons — underground infiltration gallery storage capacity. Provides peak-flow attenuation and groundwater recharge. IFC mapping: IfcDistributionSystem (domain: DRAINAGE); Uniclass: Ss_55_30 (drainage systems).

site-programming.$description: Site programming elements associated with the water management and sustainable landscaping design.

pedestrian-circulation.$description: Pedestrian circulation integrated with bioswale and permaculture landscaping zones. Paths separate from vehicular access per site programming diagram.

bike-circulation.$description: Bike circulation routes integrated with pedestrian paths and landscaping. Supports active transportation modal split for Professional Centre tenants.

site-materials-standard.$description: Site materials selected for environmental performance: permeable pavers over standard asphalt, Buffalo Grass over irrigated turf, bioswales over concrete drainage channels. Aligns with Permaculture as Commercial Landscaping principle (AEC_White Paper_Sample_V3.pdf).
